performance_overview: Through 45 sessions (inception 2026-06-01) Bull holds 7 open positions (SCHW, PCG, XLU, CMG, GIS, META, BMY). Account equity ~$100,784 (+0.78% since $100K inception) vs SPY -1.30% cumulative — Bull now leads the benchmark by ~2.09pts, its widest gap yet. Today (Jul 16 EOD) was the standout counter-trend day: net +$95.72 (+0.10%) while SPY fell -0.82%, a +0.92% single-day alpha with zero exits. Unrealized book is broadly healthy: GIS +7.46% (best), SCHW +4.73% (anchor), BMY +2.95% (Day 0), PCG +2.44%, META flat (0.0%, faded from +2.83%); only XLU (-0.51%) and CMG (-2.78%) are red, CMG the clear laggard but locked in earnings blackout.
best_signals: RSI 55-60 at entry: 2/2 wins (100%), avg +2.15% — the ONLY consistently profitable bucket for the LEGACY intraday RSI strategy; n=2, thin sample, informational only now that the strategy is MA-20/60 trend-following. Do not act on it without n>=10.

Shallow-cross probation entries are outperforming expectations under trend-following: GIS (0.29% cross, entered 75% unit) now +7.46% and BMY (0.13% cross, entered 50% unit) +2.95% Day 0. Early positive read that a heavy size-down — NOT a skip — is the right call for shallow-but-non-earnings-adjacent golden crosses. Still only 2-3 samples; keep sizing down, do not upsize yet.
avoid: Shallow EMA cross entries on earnings-adjacent names: TSLA (Jul 7 entry, 0.06% cross spread, earnings Jul 22) lost -4.78% on exit. Rule: SKIP (do not size down) when BOTH shallow cross spread (<0.5%) AND earnings within 14 days apply simultaneously. This is the live rule under the current trend strategy.

Very shallow golden crosses (<0.1% EMA-20/60 spread): NVDA (0.07%) skipped Jul 15 as a near-clone of the failed TSLA pattern (0.06%). Treat sub-0.1% crosses as noise-level, high reversal risk — skip on sight. Note BMY at 0.13% cleared this line by a hair and was taken at half unit — it is working (+2.95%), validating 0.1% as the skip floor rather than a wider band.

LEGACY intraday RSI buckets (informational, strategy retired): RSI 60-65 0/3, RSI 65-75 0/1, gap-up opens with RSI>60 — all losers historically. Retained only as a record of why the intraday RSI approach was abandoned in favor of trend-following.
recent_market_context: Jul 10-16: MA-20/60 trend strategy running steadily; 7 positions held, book net positive on unrealized. VIX 15.67 (open Jul 16, low/calm). Notable Jul 16: broad market red (SPY -0.82%) but Bull's low-beta / defensive-leaning book (utilities, consumer staples, healthcare) held up and finished green — the trend book's diversification is paying off on down days. No death crosses triggered recently. check_earnings.py verified working again (Jul 15 evening scan: 0 errors / 15 lookups); the intermittent yfinance-TLS block from this cloud IP appears resolved, so earnings risk is script-verifiable again.

Day 12; stop $44.37. Still red but improved from -0.92%; defensive utility held up on a down-tape day. Lingering death-cross watch — no trend progress after 12 days.

Day 0 — NEW Jul 16. 50% unit (85 of 171 full), heavy size-down on the shallowest cross yet (0.13% EMA20/60 spread). Stop $55.76 (2xATR=1.4723). Fill $58.71. +2.95% intraday Day 0 — encouraging early read. Earnings confirmed clear within 14d.
